clinical_study.brief_title: Pembrolizumab Added to Adjuvant FOLFOX in Stage III MSI-H Colon Cancer (PEMBRO-FOX)
clinical_study.official_title: A Phase 2 Randomized Trial of Modified FOLFOX6 With or Without Pembrolizumab as Adjuvant Therapy in Patients With Stage III Microsatellite Instability-High (MSI-H) Colon Adenocarcinoma

brief_summary.textblock: This trial evaluates the addition of pembrolizumab (anti-PD-1 immunotherapy) to standard adjuvant modified FOLFOX6 chemotherapy in patients with Stage III (pT1-4, N1-2, M0) microsatellite instability-high (MSI-H) colon adenocarcinoma who have undergone curative surgical resection, with the hypothesis that immune checkpoint blockade combined with chemotherapy will improve disease-free survival over chemotherapy alone.

detailed_description.textblock: MSI-H colorectal cancers have an immunogenic tumor microenvironment characterized by high mutational burden and robust tumor-infiltrating lymphocyte infiltration, making them uniquely sensitive to immune checkpoint inhibition. In the metastatic MSI-H CRC setting, pembrolizumab demonstrated superiority over chemotherapy as first-line therapy (KEYNOTE-177). Whether checkpoint inhibition adds benefit to adjuvant chemotherapy in Stage III MSI-H colon cancer remains unknown. This study randomizes patients who have undergone R0 resection of Stage III colon cancer with MSI-H status to mFOLFOX6 x 12 cycles + pembrolizumab 200 mg Q3W x 17 doses (approximately 1 year) versus mFOLFOX6 x 12 cycles alone. Randomization is stratified by T stage (T1-3 vs T4), N stage (N1 vs N2), sidedness (right vs left), and BRAF mutation status (V600E vs WT). Primary endpoint is 3-year disease-free survival. KRAS/NRAS mutation status is collected as exploratory biomarker.

secondary_outcome.time_frame: During treatment period, approximately 6 months for mFOLFOX6 and 12 months for pembrolizumab

secondary_outcome.measure: Ctcular tumor DNA (ctDNA) clearance at 3 and 6 months post-surgery

criteria.textblock:
Inclusion Criteria: - Histologically confirmed colon adenocarcinoma (primary tumor arising proximal to the peritoneal reflection; rectal cancer excluded) - AJCC 8th edition pathologic Stage III (any T, N1-2, M0): pT1-4a/b, N1a/b/c or N2a/b, M0 - MSI-H status confirmed by IHC (loss of at least one mismatch repair protein: MLH1, PMS2, MSH2, MSH6) or PCR-based MSI testing (MSI-High) - Tumors may be sporadic MSI-H or Lynch syndrome-associated; Lynch syndrome testing by germline analysis is encouraged but not required for enrollment - R0 (complete) surgical resection with adequate surgical margins confirmed pathologically - Enrollment within 8 weeks of surgical resection - No prior systemic therapy for colorectal cancer (neoadjuvant therapy not applicable for colon cancer) - Age >= 18 years - ECOG Performance Status 0-1 - Adequate bone marrow: ANC >= 1500/mm3, platelets >= 100,000/mm3, hemoglobin >= 9 g/dL - Adequate hepatic function: total bilirubin <= 1.5x ULN (except Gilbert syndrome: <= 3x ULN), AST/ALT <= 2.5x ULN - Adequate renal function: creatinine clearance >= 50 mL/min (Cockcroft-Gault) - No active autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppressive therapy within 2 years - No history of colitis, inflammatory bowel disease (Crohn's disease, ulcerative colitis), or other immune-mediated gastrointestinal conditions - Negative pregnancy test for women of childbearing potential; adequate contraception required - Signed informed consent
Exclusion Criteria: - Rectal cancer (tumor located within 12 cm of anal verge or below the peritoneal reflection) - MSS (microsatellite stable) or MSI-L (low) tumors - Stage I, II, or IV colorectal cancer - Evidence of distant metastatic disease (M1) at time of surgery; peritoneal deposits classified as M1c are excluded - Prior systemic chemotherapy or immunotherapy for colorectal cancer - Active inflammatory bowel disease - Active autoimmune disease requiring systemic treatment (corticosteroids, immunosuppressants) within 2 years; replacement therapy (e.g., thyroid hormone, insulin) is allowed - Prior immune checkpoint inhibitor therapy (anti-PD-1, anti-PD-L1, anti-CTLA-4) - Active or prior documented hepatitis B or C infection (HBsAg positive, anti-HCV positive with detectable HCV RNA) - Active tuberculosis - Live vaccine administration within 30 days prior to enrollment - Peripheral neuropathy >= Grade 2 at baseline (CTCAE v5.0) - Known dihydropyrimidine dehydrogenase (DPD) deficiency (if 5-FU sensitivity testing performed) - Concurrent malignancy other than adequately treated non-melanoma skin cancer or cervical carcinoma in situ - Pregnancy or breastfeeding - Known hypersensitivity to pembrolizumab, oxaliplatin, leucovorin, or 5-fluorouracil

intervention.description: Modified FOLFOX6 (oxaliplatin 85 mg/m2 IV + leucovorin 400 mg/m2 IV + 5-fluorouracil 400 mg/m2 IV bolus + 5-FU 2400 mg/m2 continuous infusion over 46 hours) every 2 weeks for 12 cycles, plus pembrolizumab 200 mg IV every 3 weeks for 17 doses (approximately 1 year).
Modified FOLFOX6 (oxaliplatin 85 mg/m2 IV + leucovorin 400 mg/m2 IV + 5-fluorouracil 400 mg/m2 IV bolus + 5-FU 2400 mg/m2 continuous infusion over 46 hours) every 2 weeks for 12 cycles.
